title: Yosys doesn't fail on invalid argument given to abc command
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

I was trying to run the following command;
```
yosys -p "synth; abc -lut 4; opt_clean; write_blif -attr -cname -conn -impltf -param build/testarch/2x4/vlut.eblif" vlut.v
```

But accidentally screwed up and had the following command line;
```
yosys -p "synth; abc -lut 4 opt_clean; write_blif -attr -cname -conn -impltf -param build/testarch/2x4/vlut.eblif" vlut.v
```

## Expected behavior

Yosys fails with an error.

## Actual behavior

Yosys runs and doesn't seem to complain in any way? 

As far as I can tell, `opt_clean` isn't a valid argument to the `abc` command. There isn't anything named `opt_clean` inside my verilog to be selected either?

The log output looks like;

```verilog
// 4-input LUT test.
module top(input [3:0] I, output O);
    always @(I)
    case(I)
        4'b1000 : O = 1;
        4'b1001 : O = 1;
        4'b1010 : O = 1;
        4'b1100 : O = 1;
        4'b1110 : O = 1;
        4'b1111 : O = 1;
        default : O = 0;
    endcase
endmodule // top
```
